title: San Diego Boat Rental Guide: Costs, Seasons and What to Know for 2026
html:
San Diego's 112-kilometre coastline and year-round mild climate make it one of America's finest boating destinations. From Shelter Island you can rent a powerboat, catamaran or yacht for a half-day or a week; prices for a bareboat start at around USD 350 and a crewed charter can exceed USD 2,500. BoatHire24 brings together verified owners and operators for every type of trip.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in San Diego?

Prices vary widely by boat type and season, but you can expect to pay from USD 100 per person for a shared tour to over USD 4,000 for a day on a luxury motor yacht. A small self-drive motorboat typically costs USD 350, 550 for a four-hour rental. A 40-foot catamaran for a full-day charter will run USD 1,200, 1,800. Crewed yachts with a skipper and fuel included start around USD 1,500 for half a day. For the most accurate and up-to-date pricing, compare verified boat listings and book directly through day charters in San Diego on the platform.

Where to set off in San Diego

San Diego Bay and Mission Bay are the two main boating areas, each with several well-equipped marinas. Popular departure points include Shelter Island, Sun Harbor Marina, Cabrillo Isle Marina and Coronado Ferry Landing. From these you can cruise past the USS Midway Museum, explore Glorietta Bay or cross to Coronado Island. Further north, Mission Bay offers sheltered waters ideal for families and watersports. For an ocean adventure, head out through Point Loma towards the Kelp Beds or the Coronado Islands (about 15 nautical miles offshore).

Which boat to rent in San Diego

Your choice depends on your group size, budget and whether you want to drive yourself or have a skipper. BoatHire24 lists hundreds of options in San Diego. Here are the most popular types:

  1. Bowrider or centre console, ideal for a couple or small family; day trips around the bay; up to 6 guests; from USD 350 for 4 hours.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Fairline, Sunseeker), luxury and speed for parties of 8, 12; coastal cruising or sunset cruises; from USD 1,200 for 4 hours. See the Fairline charter page for a popular 50-footer based at Shelter Island.
  3. Sailing catamaran (e.g. Lagoon), stable, spacious, great for groups of 8, 12; full-day trips to the Coronado Islands; from USD 1,500 for 8 hours.
  4. Pontoon boat, relaxed cruising on Mission Bay; up to 12 guests; from USD 400 for 4 hours.
  5. Crewed yacht (e.g. Azimut, Astondoa), all-inclusive with a captain and mate; up to 12 guests; from USD 2,500 for 4 hours.

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in San Diego?

In California, you need a Boater Education Card to operate a motorboat (including a rental) if you were born on or after 1 January 1978. Renters born before that date do not need a licence for a rental, but the operator (owner or skipper) will confirm exact requirements at booking. All first-time renters are given a safety briefing. If you charter a crewed boat, the skipper holds all necessary licences.

When is the best season to rent a boat in San Diego?

The high season runs from May through September, when daytime temperatures average 21, 26°C and the sea is calmest. In summer, the prevailing north-westerly wind (the 'sea breeze') picks up by midday, often reaching 10, 15 knots, so mornings are best for smooth waters. Spring and autumn offer comfortable conditions with lighter winds. Winter rentals are possible but can be cooler and bumpier; however, whale-watching trips are popular from December to April. Book well ahead for summer weekends, as availability drops quickly.

Local tip: In August, the San Diego Bay often hosts the 'Summer Sailing Festival', check local marina noticeboards for free open days and discounted charter rates.

At a glance: San Diego boat rental

  • Price range: USD 100, 4,500 per trip
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours to full day
  • Maximum guests (bareboat): up to 12
  • Currency: USD
  • Licence: Boater Education Card required for operators born after 1 Jan 1978

Questions about boat rental in San Diego

Can I rent a boat without a captain?

Yes, you can rent a self-drive boat if you meet the licence requirements and have passed the safety briefing.

What is the minimum age to rent a boat in San Diego?

The minimum age to operate a rental boat is 18, though some operators require the renter to be 21 for larger vessels.

Are pets allowed on boat rentals in San Diego?

Many owners allow well-behaved pets, but you must check the individual boat listing and pay any additional deposit.

Can I get a refund if the weather is bad?

Most operators offer a full refund or reschedule if the charter is cancelled due to unsafe weather conditions.

Whether you want a quick afternoon cruise around Mission Bay or an all-day fishing expedition to the Coronado Islands, booking through the BoatHire24 marketplace gives you direct access to San Diego's best skippers and boat owners. If you own a boat on this coast, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of rental guests planning their 2026 trips.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in San Diego and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
